<think> The question mentions a German field marshal of the "Luftwaffe", which is the German air force. The Luftwaffe was established in 1935, so the field marshal in question is likely from World War II. However, the question asks about a First World War fighter ace, which suggests that the field marshal is a distant cousin of someone who was a fighter ace in World War I. A famous German field marshal of the Luftwaffe in World War II was Hermann Göring, who was a distant cousin of Manfred von Richthofen, a famous World War I fighter ace known as the "Red Baron". Manfred von Richthofen was credited with 80 victories, but the question asks about a pilot with 40 victories. Another famous German World War I fighter ace with 40 victories is Werner Voss. Since Hermann Göring was a distant cousin of Manfred von Richthofen, it is likely that Werner Voss is the distant cousin of a German field marshal of the Luftwaffe mentioned in the question. </think>
<answer> Werner Voss </answer>
